overview: Build a pass-the-phone React party game where one player is the guesser and all other players receive hidden improv roles tied to a shared situation. The guesser can say a guess out loud at any time and then reveal the answer with confirmation to check win or loss.
goals: Support 4+ players with one guesser and at least three mandatory performer roles
Keep each player's role reveal private during handoff
Provide reusable situation packs with mandatory and optional roles so groups of different sizes can play
Enable fast replay with the same players after each round
Situation chosen at random from pre-made situations when Random is selected
Allow each player to privately re-check their own role during live play if they forget it
Ship with 10 curated situations (the original 5 plus 5 generated scenarios)
nonGoals: No online multiplayer or real-time networking
No user accounts, matchmaking, or cloud persistence
No scoring system in this version
No AI-generated roles or situations in this version
successMetrics: A game can start from setup to first role reveal in under 60 seconds for 4-6 players
100% of rounds assign exactly one guesser and valid role cards to all remaining players
No player can view another player's role unless the device is physically shared
Every reveal action (role reveal and final answer reveal) requires explicit confirmation before hidden content is shown
The catalog contains exactly 10 playable situations with enough clue signals for the guesser to infer the setting
Build completes successfully with no runtime errors in local smoke testing
openQuestions: Should rounds include a configurable timer for guessing?
Should hosts be able to create custom situations in-app or only via JSON files?
Should we track multiple guess attempts or only final reveal outcome?
Should the game support multiple languages at launch?

stack.hosting: Static hosting (Vercel, Netlify, or Cloudflare Pages)

routes.name: Single-Page Game
routes.purpose: Runs setup, private reveal flow, and live play state transitions in one route

uiNotes: Mobile-first layout with large tap targets because the device is passed between players
Reveal flow must clearly separate pass, confirm identity, and reveal stages
Role text should be centered and compact to reduce shoulder-surfing during reveal
Error states should be explicit (duplicate names, too few players, incompatible situation)
Every hidden-content action includes a second explicit confirm step
Live phase must include a private 'Show My Role Again' flow with the same pass and confirm safeguards

importFormat.description: JSON format for loading the full V1 situation catalog.

roles.name: Blue - Injured Dog

roles.prompt: Use sound effects and mic checks, no normal dialogue.

rules: Minimum players is 4 (1 guesser + 3 mandatory performer roles).
Exactly one guesser is chosen at random each round.
Selected situation must support player count with performers = players - 1 and mandatoryRoles <= performers <= totalRoles.
All mandatory roles are included before optional roles are sampled.
Player names are required, trimmed, and case-insensitively unique.
Role and guesser prompts may contain template tokens such as {player} and {guesser}.
All hidden content actions require explicit confirmation before reveal (role reveal, role re-check, and final answer reveal).
During live play, the guesser can say a guess out loud at any time, then reveal the answer to confirm win/loss.
During live play, each player may privately re-check only their own role.
Every situation must include at least one explicit role interaction and enough clue signals for the guesser to infer the setting.

stories.title: Define situation and role domain model
stories.description: As a developer, I want a clear in-code model for situations and roles so round generation can enforce player-count rules reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Create structured situation objects with id, name, guesser prompt, clue checklist, and roles with mandatory flags.
Role schema includes communicationMode, interactionWith, and clueSignals.
Example: with 5 players and a situation containing 3 mandatory + 2 optional roles, the situation is valid because 4 performers can be assigned.
Negative case: with 4 players and a situation requiring 4 mandatory performer roles, the situation is rejected because only 3 performers are available.
Show a deterministic error message when no situation is compatible.

stories.title: Build setup flow for players and situation selection
stories.description: As a host, I want to enter player names and choose a specific or random situation so I can start a valid round quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide input rows for player names with add/remove controls and enforce minimum 4 players.
Provide a situation selector with a Random option that chooses only compatible situations.
Display setup status including ready player count, minimum required, and max supported for selected situation.
Example: entering 4 unique names and choosing Random enables starting a round.
Negative case: attempting to start with duplicate names like 'Alex' and 'alex' shows a validation error and blocks start.

stories.title: Generate round assignments with one guesser
stories.description: As a player, I want fair random assignment of guesser and performer roles so each round feels different.
acceptanceCriteria: Randomly choose exactly one guesser from the current players.
Assign all remaining players to role cards, always including mandatory roles and then optional roles as needed.
Render guesser and role prompt templates with token replacement.
Example: for 6 players, 1 guesser is selected and 5 performers receive roles where all mandatory roles appear exactly once.
Negative case: if no compatible situation exists, round creation fails with a clear user-facing error.

stories.title: Implement private pass-and-reveal handoff with confirmation
stories.description: As a participant, I want a protected reveal flow so only I can view my role before passing the device onward.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement reveal stages per player: pass screen, identity confirmation, reveal request, and confirm reveal.
Role text remains hidden until the active player confirms reveal.
Provide Hide and Continue to advance to the next player until all cards are seen.
Example: Player 2 cannot see role text until pressing Confirm Reveal on their turn.
Negative case: canceling at confirmation keeps role text hidden.

stories.title: Support live phase with guess-anytime answer resolution
stories.description: As the guesser, I want to say a guess out loud at any point and then reveal the answer so the game can resolve correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: After the last player reveal, transition to live phase and show guesser identity.
Provide a control for the guesser to lock in a spoken guess and trigger answer reveal.
Require confirmation before revealing the true situation and computing win/loss.
Example: guesser says 'Vet Clinic', confirms reveal, and UI shows whether the guess is correct.
Negative case: if confirmation is not given, the actual situation remains hidden.

stories.title: Allow private role re-check during live play
stories.description: As a performer, I want to privately re-open my own role during the scene if I forget details.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a live-phase 'Show My Role Again' flow with pass and confirmation safeguards.
Re-check reveal requires explicit confirmation before showing role text.
Hide returns safely to live phase after re-check.
Example: Purple re-checks only Purple's card and returns to acting.
Negative case: no player can reveal another player's role through re-check flow.

stories.title: Seed catalog with the five original situations
stories.description: As a host, I want the original five scenarios from the concept so the game preserves the intended experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Include Animal Shelter, Vet Clinic, Pirate Treasure, Stormy Night At Sea, and Sushi Restaurant.
Each situation defines at least 3 mandatory performer roles and optional roles for larger groups.
Each includes role interactions and clue checklist entries.
Example: Animal Shelter includes injured dog and happy cat interacting with a caretaker.
Negative case: any situation with fewer than 3 mandatory roles is excluded.

stories.title: Add five generated situations with communication constraints
stories.description: As a host, I want five extra creative scenarios that use draw-only, mime-only, and talk/mime roles plus other wild constraints.
acceptanceCriteria: Include Haunted Museum Heist, Space Station Oxygen Leak, Wedding Cake Disaster, Volcano Research Camp, and Movie Set Meltdown.
Across these situations, include roles with draw_only, mime_only, talk_mime, and at least one of sound_only/silent/gibberish_only.
Each generated situation has at least one explicit role-to-role interaction and clue checklist supporting guesser inference from performance alone.
Example: in Space Station Oxygen Leak, one role draws system diagrams while another mimes oxygen distress and interacts with an engineer.
Negative case: reject a generated situation if the guess depends on saying hidden keywords instead of performable clues.

stories.title: Polish mobile usability and accessibility
stories.description: As a party player, I want clear and accessible controls on a phone so pass-around flow is smooth in noisy environments.
acceptanceCriteria: Use responsive single-column layout on small screens with readable typography and high contrast controls.
Ensure all actions are keyboard reachable and controls have labels.
Support long player names without overlapping controls.
Example: on a 390px-wide viewport, reveal and confirmation controls remain usable without horizontal scrolling.
Negative case: role text never flashes during screen transitions.

stories.title: Document local run and gameplay rules
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want concise setup and gameplay docs so others can run and understand the game quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Update README with install/run steps and short gameplay flow.
Document minimum player rule, guess-at-any-time resolution, reveal confirmations, role re-check flow, and restart options.
List all 10 situations by name.
Example: README includes `npm install` and `npm run dev` plus a numbered how-to-play section with guess resolution.
Negative case: if docs omit any situation name or verification command, the story is not accepted.
